easy: Compute a simple moving average of closing prices (default window = 10).
Generate cumulative return from the close-price series (default horizon = 20).
Create a factor equal to today’s close divided by its rolling mean close (default window = 15).
Produce percentage change in closing price over the recent look-back (default window = 5).
Return the high-to-low ratio for the current bar (single-day metric).
Measure the intraday move as close minus open (single-day metric).
Output the rolling maximum of closing prices (default window = 30).
Compute the rolling minimum of closing prices (default window = 30).
Average daily high–low range across the preset span (default window = 12).
Calculate close minus its simple moving average (default window = 8).
Form a price ratio: close divided by its rolling minimum (default window = 14).
Sum close-to-close returns across the built-in short horizon (default window = 6).
Capture today’s trading range magnitude as high minus low (single-day metric).
Average the low price over the baseline period (default window = 9).
Return the ratio of close to high for the current session (single-day metric).
Compute rolling standard deviation of closing prices (default window = 18).
Identify the rolling maximum of the high price series (default window = 25).
Identify the rolling minimum of the low price series (default window = 25).
Sum open-to-close differences over the short horizon (default window = 7).
Provide close minus its exponential moving average (default span = 13).
Average the open price using the framework’s baseline look-back (default window = 20).
Compute close relative to its rolling average close (default window = 40).
Average daily high–low spreads over the preset period (default window = 16).
Create a factor: high minus previous close (single-day metric).
Calculate the rolling range of closing prices (max-min) (default window = 22).
Compute close divided by low over a brief horizon (default window = 2).
Average absolute close-to-open change (default window = 5).
Sum daily high-to-low ratios across the look-back (default window = 10).
Compute rolling variance of open prices (default window = 12).
Average the high-to-low ratio over time (default window = 14).
Generate a simple moving average of close prices (default window = 28).
Calculate close minus its rolling minimum (default window = 12).
Average open-to-high differences over the default span (default window = 20).
Total intraday ranges for recent bars (default window = 9).
Return close divided by its long-term SMA (default window = 50).
Identify the rolling maximum of open prices (default window = 21).
Compute close minus low for the current session (single-day metric).
Identify the rolling minimum of the high price (default window = 17).
Average intraday range using the built-in look-back (default window = 30).
Return open divided by open at the prior step (default lag = 10).
Sum daily close-to-close changes across the short horizon (default window = 8).
Average the high-minus-close spread (default window = 6).
Compute close relative to its weekly mean (default window = 5).
Average the low price across recent sessions (default window = 3).
Track the rolling max of close-minus-open differences (default window = 12).
Track the rolling min of high-minus-close differences (default window = 24).
Shifted simple moving average of closing prices (default window = 15).
Return the close-to-previous-close ratio (single-day metric).
Accumulate open-price returns across recent bars (default window = 18).
Average absolute high-low range (default window = 4).
Compute close minus its rolling median close (default window = 20).
Average open-to-low differences (default window = 25).
Track the rolling max of range percentages (default window = 10).
Sum close minus previous open across bars (default window = 5).
Average the high-to-low price ratio (default window = 7).
Calculate rolling variance of closing prices (default window = 14).
Compute close relative to its long-term trend line (default window = 200).
Median of open prices across the preset span (default window = 9).
Close minus its rolling mean (default window = 30).
Rolling standard deviation of high prices (default window = 6).
Rolling max of open-to-close returns (default window = 13).
Average high prices across recent periods (default window = 22).
Average absolute close-to-open gaps (default window = 20).
High minus yesterday’s close as a signal (single-day metric).
Cumulative percentage change of the low price (default horizon = 4).
Close relative to recent maximum close (default window = 30).
Rolling minimum of the open price (default window = 11).
Average high-to-open difference (default window = 19).
Rolling sum of high minus open (default window = 8).
Average daily close minus low (default window = 12).
Close relative to its distant past value (default lag = 60).
Average intraday closing momentum (close-open) (default window = 5).
Low minus its rolling minimum low (default window = 10).
Rolling mean of high prices (default window = 7).
Rolling variance of open prices (default window = 21).
Close minus its rolling maximum (default window = 3).
Average daily return of closing prices (default window = 11).
Compute high divided by low for the session (single-day metric).
Sum of absolute open-to-open returns (default window = 6).
Mean of high minus low differences (default window = 17).
Close relative to average high price (default window = 9).
Rolling mean absolute deviation of close (default window = 15).
Open divided by recent rolling max open (default window = 20).
Close minus the rolling median high (default window = 18).
medium: Compute cumulative closing-price return divided by mean volume over the same period (default window = 10).
Generate a factor that multiplies daily close-to-close return by relative volume ratio (today's volume / average volume, default volume window = 5).
Create a signal equal to close minus its moving average, then scale by rolling standard deviation of volume (default price window = 15, volume window = 15).
Produce the difference between fast and slow simple moving averages of close, weighted by current volume (default short = 5, long = 20).
Return close-to-open change divided by average true range and multiplied by volume percentile (default ATR window = 14).
Calculate the ratio of cumulative intraday range sum to cumulative volume sum (default window = 12).
Design a factor where daily return is multiplied by z-scored volume (default z-score window = 20).
Compute high minus low, then divide by rolling median volume (default window = 8).
Provide a price breakout indicator—close above rolling max—scaled by volume spike size (default price window = 30, volume spike window = 30).
Generate a momentum factor (close / lagged close) and adjust by log(volume) difference (default lag = 3).
Create a signal that ranks securities by close-to-close return and multiplies by ranked volume (default rank window = 7).
Calculate rolling correlation between close returns and volume changes (default window = 20).
Return intraday move (close − open) normalized by rolling mean absolute volume (default window = 10).
Produce high-low range divided by rolling volume standard deviation (default window = 15).
Compute exponential moving average of close, subtract current close, and weight by volume percentile (default span = 10).
Design a factor equal to cumulative positive returns divided by cumulative negative volume change (default window = 12).
Generate a price gap (open − prev close) multiplied by current volume / average volume (default volume window = 5).
Create a signal using RSI of closing price, then scale by rolling z-score of volume (default RSI length = 14, z-score window = 14).
Return the rolling max of close minus current close, divided by volume SMA (default window = 25).
Compute the slope of close prices via linear regression and multiply by rolling mean volume (default window = 9).
Provide cumulative high minus close summed over days, divided by cumulative volume (default window = 18).
Calculate Bollinger band width of close and adjust by volume spike ratio (default band window = 20).
Design a factor: (close − SMA(close)) / SMA(volume) (default window = 10).
Generate rolling beta between close returns and volume changes (default window = 22).
Compute deviation of VWAP from close and multiply by normalized volume (default VWAP window = 10).
Return cross of two EMAs of close weighted by volume growth (default short = 6, long = 18).
Produce cumulative sum of (close − open) × sign(volume − SMA(volume)) (default window = 15).
Create the ratio of current high to rolling max high, scaled by volume percentile (default window = 12).
Calculate price volatility (std of returns) divided by std of volume (default window = 20).
Generate a factor where rolling min close distance is multiplied by rolling volume skewness (default window = 14).
Provide intraday range percentage multiplied by relative volume (default volume window = 3).
Compute EMA of volume and divide close-to-close return by this EMA (default span = 8).
Design a signal equal to rolling average of (close / low) times z-score of log volume (default window = 11).
Create the difference between close and VWAP, divided by rolling max volume (default window = 25).
Return rolling Sharpe of close returns multiplied by rolling coefficient of variation of volume (default window = 30).
Generate cumulative product of (1 + return) and subtract cumulative mean volume ratio (default window = 10).
Compute MACD (default fast = 12, slow = 26) and weight by volume percentile (default volume window = 12).
Produce sum of positive intraday moves when volume > SMA(volume) minus sum when volume < SMA(volume) (default window = 9).
Return rolling quantile rank of close and subtract rolling quantile rank of volume (default window = 20).
Calculate close divided by rolling median close and multiply by volume z-score (default window = 16).
Design a factor equal to rolling ATR divided by log(volume) (default ATR window = 14).
Generate rolling maximum drawdown of close weighted by cumulative volume (default window = 25).
Compute the ratio of current volume to rolling max volume, times price momentum (default momentum lag = 5).
Provide slope of log(close) and multiply by percentile rank of today’s volume (default slope window = 7).
Create EMA of high-low range divided by EMA of volume (default span = 10).
Return rolling kurtosis of price returns scaled by mean volume (default window = 21).
Produce (close − low) / (high − low) multiplied by relative volume (default volume window = 4).
Calculate rolling standard deviation of intraday gaps normalized by volume std (default window = 12).
Design a signal equal to rolling covariance between return and volume change (default window = 18).
Generate cumulative signed volume (sign(return) × volume) divided by cumulative absolute volume (default window = 15).
Compute distance of close from rolling median and divide by volume coefficient of variation (default window = 20).
Provide RSI of close weighted by volume spike factor (default RSI length = 14, spike window = 14).
Create percentile rank of intraday range and add percentile rank of volume (default window = 10).
Return rolling z-score of close divided by rolling z-score of volume (default window = 12).
Produce sum of daily returns when volume is above its 75th percentile minus sum when below 25th (default window = 20).
Compute log(close) change multiplied by log(volume) change (default lag = 1).
Generate SMA of close / SMA of volume and subtract one (default window = 30).
Design a factor: (high − low)/close multiplied by volume ratio (default window = 8).
Calculate crossing signal of price SMA lines filtered by volume uptrend (default short = 5, long = 15, volume trend window = 10).
Return average of intraday returns when volume z-score > 0 (default window = 12).
Create rolling min distance (close − rolling min) divided by volume percentile (default window = 16).
Produce ATR normalized by average volume and multiplied by sign(return) (default ATR window = 14).
Compute rolling max of close relative to SMA(close) weighted by volume decay factor (default window = 20).
Generate price momentum (close / lagged close − 1) divided by log(volume) (default lag = 4).
Provide linear regression beta of price on volume for recent bars (default window = 10).
Design a factor equal to cumulative high minus rolling median high, scaled by avg volume (default window = 18).
Calculate VWAP deviation normalized by volume volatility (default volume window = 15).
Return sum of (open − low) weighted by volume percentile (default window = 9).
Create rolling variance of high prices divided by rolling mean volume (default window = 21).
Produce moving average convergence of volume and multiply by price rank (default short = 5, long = 20).
Compute ratio of current volume to rolling mean and multiply by (close − SMA(close)) (default window = 12).
Generate rolling skewness of returns normalized by median volume (default window = 22).
Design a factor: price z-score × volume z-score (default window = 14).
Calculate percentage distance from close to rolling max close and weight by volume spike (default window = 25).
Return cumulative sum of (high − close) when volume > SMA(volume) (default window = 15).
Produce sum of signed volume (return sign) over rolling window divided by length (default window = 10).
Compute variance of intraday range normalized by EMA(volume) (default span = 12).
Provide EMA of close returns multiplied by EMA of volume changes (default span = 8).
Create rolling MAD (mean absolute deviation) of close divided by volume percentile (default window = 18).
Generate price gap divided by rolling ATR and scaled by volume ratio (default ATR window = 14).
Design a factor where Bollinger band position is multiplied by log(volume) (default window = 20).
Calculate cross-sectional rank of return times cross-sectional rank of volume (default window = 1 day).
Return ratio of current close to VWAP multiplied by relative volume (default volume window = 6).
Create signal: (close − low)/(high − low) minus rolling mean, times volume percentile (default window = 12).
Produce truncated z-score of close and add truncated z-score of volume (default window = 20).
Calculate cumulative return over two sub-windows and weight by cumulative volume over same sub-windows (default short = 5, long = 20).
Design an oscillator: SMA(close) − SMA(close, longer) divided by SMA(volume) (default short = 7, long = 30).
Generate price momentum divided by standard deviation of volume (default window = 14).
Provide rolling max drawup of close weighted by recent volume spike (default window = 22).
Compute exponential decay sum of returns where weight = relative volume (default window = 10).
Create correlation between price momentum and volume momentum (default window = 12).
Return ATR times volume percentile minus rolling median price change (default ATR window = 14).
Produce SMA(high − low) divided by SMA(volume) and take its z-score (default window = 16).
Calculate (close − open) × (volume − SMA(volume)) sign-adjusted (default window = 5).
Generate cumulative product of (1 + return) weighted by volume growth factor (default window = 15).
Design a factor: rolling variance of close minus rolling variance of volume (default window = 18).
Compute RSI of price and divide by RSI of volume (default length = 14).
Return distance of close from EMA(close) multiplied by volume spike ratio (default span = 10).
Create rolling min low distance normalized by rolling mean volume (default window = 20).
Produce SMA of close returns conditional on volume above rolling median (default window = 12).
Calculate logistic transform of price z-score times volume percentile (default window = 14).
Generate alpha equal to (close / SMA(close) − 1) × (volume / SMA(volume) − 1) (default window = 10).
Provide rolling covariance of high-low range and volume change (default window = 18).
Design signal: close percentile rank minus volume percentile rank (default window = 25).
Compute price slope via regression and divide by volume slope (default regression window = 9).
Return ATR divided by rolling mean of volume and multiplied by price momentum (default ATR window = 14).
Create rolling harmonic mean of close weighted by volume percentile (default window = 13).
Produce cumulative open-to-high difference summed when volume increases (default window = 11).
Calculate coefficient of variation of close times coefficient of variation of volume (default window = 20).
Generate median of (close − open) multiplied by median volume ratio (default window = 7).
Design factor: exponential moving average of positive returns divided by EMA of volume (default span = 12).
Compute rolling rank of price gap times rolling rank of volume change (default window = 15).
Return difference between price SMA crossover count and volume SMA crossover count (default short = 5, long = 20).
hard: Design a factor that ranks stocks by z-scored 10-day cumulative return, then multiplies the rank by the inverse of rolling volume volatility (default return window = 10, volume vol window = 20).

Generate a signal that outputs 1 if close is above its 30-day rolling maximum and today’s volume exceeds its 90th percentile, otherwise −1; the result is then standardized cross-sectionally each day (default price window = 30, volume window = 30).
Create a factor equal to the beta of 15-day price returns regressed on 15-day volume changes, divided by the rolling standard deviation of those betas (default window = 15).

Compute the percentage distance of close from its 60-day rolling median, winsorize at ±3σ, multiply by volume percentile rank, and finally rank the result cross-sectionally (default median window = 60).
Construct an indicator that counts consecutive up closes longer than 3 days while volume is below its 20-day SMA; output the count divided by rolling ATR (default ATR window = 14).
Return the skewness of 21-day close returns minus the skewness of 21-day log-volume changes, then divide by their pooled standard deviation (default window = 21).
Produce a factor equal to (VWAP − close) / close, multiplied by the z-score of current volume, then ranked within sector buckets (default VWAP window = 10).
Compute rolling correlation between price momentum (5-day) and volume momentum (5-day), square the correlation, multiply by sign of price momentum, and z-score the final value (default window = 20).
Generate a signal where (close − low)/(high − low) is weighted by log(volume/SMA(volume)) and then ranked across all assets (default volume window = 10).

Design a factor: if today’s close exceeds yesterday’s high and volume exceeds its 30-day median, output the z-score of the 7-day return; else output −z-score of 7-day return (default return window = 7).

Create a composite score that averages three z-scored components: price RSI(14), volume RSI(14), and relative intraday range, then multiplies by cross-sectional rank of volume spike (default RSI length = 14).
Calculate the ratio of rolling 20-day price variance to rolling 20-day volume variance, subtract its 60-day EMA, and standardize the difference (default long window = 60).
Output the negative of rolling max drawdown over 30 days divided by cumulative volume over the same horizon, then rank descending (default window = 30).
Generate a factor that computes the slope of log(close) over 10 days, multiplies by slope of log(volume) over 10 days, and divides by ATR (default ATR window = 14).
Return cross-sectional percentile of today’s gap (open−prev close) scaled by volume spike factor, then demean within industry groups (default spike window = 20).
Design a signal: ((close/SMA(close,20)) − 1) × ((volume/SMA(volume,20)) − 1), winsorized at 5th–95th percentiles and ranked every day.
Compute 12-day MACD of close, subtract 26-day MACD of close, weight by log(volume percentile), then apply a hyperbolic tan squashing function (default MACD fast/slow = 12/26).
Create a factor that triggers when price closes in the top 20 % of its 50-day range and volume is in the bottom 40 %; assign +1, else −1, then apply 5-day cumulative sum.
Calculate the rolling covariance between (high−low)/(high+low) and log(volume) over 18 days, divide by rolling variance of log(volume), and negate the result.
Return a factor: sign of 10-day price slope multiplied by √(rolling kurtosis of volume) and divided by Bollinger-band width of price (default band window = 20).
Generate cross-sectional z-score of (close − VWAP)/VWAP and multiply by cross-sectional z-score of (volume − median(volume,30))/median(volume,30).
Design a signal equal to rolling count of days where close > SMA(close,5) AND volume < SMA(volume,5), divided by total days in 21-day window.
Compute (high−close)/(high−low) weighted by (volume − rolling min volume)/(rolling max volume − rolling min volume), then rank ascending.
Create the ratio of rolling 7-day positive price change sum to 7-day negative volume change sum, then take natural log of absolute value plus 1 and z-score.
Calculate regression beta of price returns on absolute volume for the past 30 days, subtract rolling mean beta, divide by rolling std beta, and clip at ±4.
Produce a factor that is (close percentile − volume percentile) × sign(price momentum), then pass through a cubic transformation and rank.
Return difference between two cross-sectional ranks: rank of price volatility (20-day) minus rank of volume volatility (20-day).
Generate z-score of rolling max(close)/rolling min(close) over 25 days, multiplied by inverse z-score of rolling mean volume.
Design an oscillator: (EMA(close,12) − EMA(close,26))/EMA(close,12) multiplied by percentile rank of today’s volume spike (default fast/slow = 12/26).
Create a factor that sums (close − open) × sign(volume − SMA(volume,20)) over 10 days, divides by rolling ATR, and ranks.
Compute rolling 30-day downside deviation of price returns, divide by rolling upside volume deviation, then negate.
Return (close/SMA(close,40) − SMA(close,40)/SMA(close,200)) weighted by volume percentile spike (default long SMA = 200).
Calculate rolling 14-day RSI of close, adjust by (1 − volume RSI), then standardize and rank.
Produce factor: if price gapped up and closed lower, assign −z-score of gap × volume spike; else assign z-score of gap × volume spike.
Design signal equal to rolling Sharpe ratio of returns divided by rolling coefficient of variation of volume, then cube the result.
Compute rolling correlation between (close − low)/(high − low) and (volume − SMA(volume,10)), then apply exponential decay weighting (decay = 0.85).
Create cross-sectional rank of (close − rolling median close) normalized by MAD, times cross-sectional rank of volume z-score.
Generate rolling z-score of VWAP deviation, subtract rolling z-score of volume skewness, then divide by price ATR.
Design factor that counts streaks where close > SMA(close,10) AND volume > SMA(volume,10); output streak length / 10.
Compute (close − low) / (high − low) − rolling mean of same, multiply by ranked volume change, then clip at ±2.
Produce MACD histogram of log(close) and weight it by coefficient of determination (R²) of volume regression (default fast/slow = 12/26).
Create a factor equal to cumulative sum over 15 days of sign(return) × (volume − median(volume)), divided by cumulative |return|.
Return 1 / (1 + exp(−z-score of price/volume correlation)) minus 0.5, giving centered logistic output.
Design an indicator: if close within 5 % of 20-day high AND volume within bottom quartile, assign −1; if close within 5 % of 20-day low AND volume spike > 1.5×, assign +1; else 0.
Compute rolling standard deviation of (close × volume) series divided by rolling mean of the same, then log-transform and z-score.
Generate price momentum (close/lagged close − 1) divided by √ATR, multiplied by sign(volume change) and winsorized at ±3σ.
Create a factor that ranks descending the product of price drawup over 30 days and inverse drawdown of volume over 30 days.
Return rolling Hurst exponent of close minus rolling Hurst of volume, then standardize.
Produce EMA of absolute return weighted by volume percentile minus SMA of return weighted by volume SMA, then rank.
Design signal: ((close − SMA(close,20))/SMA(close,20))² × ((volume − SMA(volume,20))/SMA(volume,20)), then sin() transform.
Compute rolling Pearson R between ATR and volume volatility (14-day each), negate it, and z-score across universe.
Generate a factor that sums (high − close)/high over days where volume z-score > 1, subtracts the same sum when volume z-score < −1 (window = 12).
Create cross-sectional percentile of price gap scaled by inverse percentile of volume gap and then demean.
Return the rolling beta of (high − low)/(high + low) on log(volume) and divide by rolling idiosyncratic error std.
Design indicator: if today’s range > 1.5 × ATR AND volume spike > 2, output z-score of price change; else 0.
Compute cumulative product of (1 + sign(return) × volume percentile/100) minus 1 over 20 days, then rank.
Produce rolling mean absolute deviation of close divided by rolling mean absolute deviation of volume, take ln and standardize.
Create factor equal to (RSI(price) − 50) × (50 − RSI(volume)), then divide by 50 and apply cube.
Return the square of price momentum (5-day) minus square of volume momentum (5-day), divided by ATR.
Generate the ratio of rolling max(high) − min(low) to rolling max(volume) − min(volume) over 25 days, then invert.
Design a factor where rolling Sharpe of return > 0 raises score by z-score of volume spike; else subtract same amount.
Compute eigenvector centrality of price-volume covariance matrix (20-day) for each asset and use as factor value.
Produce percentile rank of current close within its 60-day range minus percentile rank of current volume within 60-day volume range.
Create conditional factor: if 3-day cumulative return < −3 % AND volume spike > 1.8× assign z-score(Volume) − z-score(Return); else 0.
Return ratio of rolling 14-day ATR to rolling 14-day volume MAD, then subtract long-term median of the ratio (default long window = 60).
Design signal: exponential decay (λ=0.9) weighted sum of (Sign(return) × Sign(volume change)), then divide by cumulative abs(return).
Compute rolling Kendall tau between price and volume, cube it, multiply by −1, and standardize.
Produce factor = (close − VWAP)/(high − low) × ln(volume/SMA(volume,30)), then cross-sectionally rank every Monday.
Create the ratio of price volatility to volume volatility, raise to the power of 1.5, then invert and clip at 10.
Return cumulative sum of (rank of price gap × rank of volume spike) over 14 days divided by 14.
Design an indicator that detects inside-day bars followed by volume spike; output +z-score(range) if bullish close, else −z-score(range).
Compute linear regression R-squared of close on volume for past 25 days, multiply by sign of slope, and log-transform.
Generate factor equal to (close/SMA(close,10) − SMA(close,10)/SMA(close,40)) × (volume/SMA(volume,10) − 1).
Return rolling variance of residuals from price = β × volume regression, divided by rolling variance of price.
Produce a factor that penalizes large positive price jumps that occur on low volume by assigning −|z-score(jump)| × (1 − volume percentile).
Design signal: (RSI(price) × RSI(volume) / 100) − average of previous 5 values, then rank.
Compute normalized distance of close from upper Bollinger band multiplied by normalized volume distance from its upper band.
Generate rolling count of days where price closed in top quartile of range while volume closed in bottom quartile; divide by window length (default window = 21).
Create factor: sign of (EMA(close,12) − EMA(close,26)) × log1p(volume/SMA(volume,20)), then z-score.
Return square root of (rolling variance of close) divided by cubic root of (rolling variance of volume) and rank.
Produce ratio of amplitude of 3-day price FFT component to amplitude of 3-day volume FFT component, then log-scale.
Design indicator: if rolling correlation between return and volume change < −0.3 assign +1 × z-score(return); if > 0.3 assign −1 × z-score(return).
Generate factor equal to difference between rank of price kurtosis and rank of volume skewness (window = 28).
Create composite: (close − SMA(close,20))/SMA(close,20) − (volume − SMA(volume,20))/SMA(volume,20), then square and rank.
Return ratio of rolling downside price semivariance to upside volume semivariance, minus its 90-day median.
Produce z-score of rolling beta of log(close) on log(volume), multiply by −1, and clip at ±3.
Design signal: cross-sectional rank of today’s (high − low)/ATR times (volume spike − 1), demeaned.
Compute EMA of (close − low)/(high − close) with span 7, multiply by SMA(volume,5)/SMA(volume,20), and standardize.
Generate factor: log1p(rolling sum of positive returns) − log1p(rolling sum of negative returns) all weighted by volume percentile (window = 14).
Create rank difference between price percentile within 252-day history and volume percentile within same history, then divide by 100.
Return negative of rolling max drawdown depth times z-score(volume spike) divided by days since drawdown bottom (window = 60).
Produce (close − lagged close)^2 multiplied by (volume − lagged volume)^2, take fourth-root, and z-score.
Design indicator: if close above 200-day SMA and volume above 50-day SMA, output z-score of 20-day momentum; else output −z-score of 20-day momentum.
Generate factor that sums sign(return) × rank(volume) over a 21-day exponentially decayed window (λ = 0.87).
Create cross-sectional z-score of (close − VWAP)/VWAP, raise to the power of 1.8, and multiply by −1 × sign(volume change).
Return ratio of current close to rolling max close, subtract ratio of current volume to rolling max volume, and z-score.
Produce skewness of (close × volume) series over 18 days, subtract long-term average skewness, and divide by long-term std.
Design factor: rolling median of |return| multiplied by rolling MAD of volume, then normalized by price MAD (window = 30).
Compute correlation between rank(return) and rank(volume change) using Spearman over 25 days, square it, multiply by −sign(return momentum).
Generate logistic transformation of (price RSI − volume RSI), then demean and divide by rolling std of the same (RSI length = 14).
Create a factor equal to cumulative sum over 40 days of (close − SMA(close,10)) × (volume − SMA(volume,10)), divided by rolling sum of ATR.
Return inverse of coefficient of variation of close divided by inverse coefficient of variation of volume, then log-scale and rank.
Produce difference between rolling mean of (high − low)/close and rolling mean of volume growth, standardized over 60 days.
Design an oscillator where fast SMA of (price/volume) minus slow SMA of (price/volume) is z-scored and multiplied by sign of recent return (short = 5, long = 20).
Compute rolling w%R of price multiplied by inverse rolling w%R of volume, then square root and rank.
Generate factor: (close/low − 1) divided by (high/close − 1) weighted by volume percentile, then clip at ±5 and standardize.
Create the product of Kendall tau (price, volume) and rank of ATR/price, multiply by −1 and z-score across assets.
Return exponential-weighted count of days where close in upper half of range and volume in lower half, minus count of opposite condition (decay = 0.9).
Produce rolling Z-score of price gap magnitude multiplied by normalized cumulative volume spike, then cube root.
Design a factor equal to ln(1 + cumulative positive ATR moves) minus ln(1 + cumulative negative ATR moves), weighted by volume kurtosis (window = 20).
Compute Fisher transform of (close − SMA(close,20))/SMA(close,20) and add Fisher transform of (volume − SMA(volume,20))/SMA(volume,20), then rank.
Generate rolling Sharpe ratio of (close − lagged low) divided by rolling std of volume, subtract its 90-day EMA, and z-score.
Create cross-sectional rank of 1/(1 + price volatility) × volume spike factor, demeaned each Friday.
Return negative of (close − VWAP)/VWAP when volume percentile < 20 %, else positive of same value when volume percentile > 80 %, then standardize.
Produce factor: rolling min distance (close − rolling min) / rolling min × log(volume percentile + 1), ranked descending.
Design indicator that outputs z-score of 5-day downside return only when volume variance is below its 25th percentile; otherwise 0.
Compute rolling sum of sign(return) × abs(return) × abs(volume change) divided by rolling sum of abs(return) (window = 14).
Generate ratio of ATR to (rolling high − rolling low) weighted by inverse volume percentile, then invert and z-score.
Return the beta of rank(price) on rank(volume) in a 30-day window, subtract 0.5, multiply by −2, and clip between −3 and 3.
Produce cumulative ratio of positive price gaps to negative volume gaps over 20 days, subtract 0.5, and rank.
Design factor: (SMA(close,5)/SMA(close,20) − 1) × (1 − SMA(volume,5)/SMA(volume,20)), cubed and standardized.
Compute cross-sectional percentile of current ATR divided by percentile of current volume volatility, then take natural log.
Generate rolling mean of (high − low)/(close × volume percentile) weighted by exponential decay (λ = 0.8), rank ascending.
Create a signal: if price makes new 50-day high and volume is below 50-day mean, assign −z-score(gap); if price makes new 50-day low and volume spike > 2×, assign +z-score(gap).
Return difference between rolling skewness of return and rolling kurtosis of volume, divided by pooled std and multiplied by −1.
Produce log1p cumulative volume when price volatility below long-term median minus log1p cumulative volume when volatility above median, normalized.
Design a factor that ranks (close − EMA(close,21))/EMA(close,21) × (volume − EMA(volume,21))/EMA(volume,21) every Wednesday, then carries forward.
Compute the ratio of rolling sum of (high − close) on down-volume days to sum of (close − low) on up-volume days (window = 30) and invert.
Generate fast/slow EMA crossover of price gap, weighted by variance ratio of volume (fast = 3, slow = 10), then z-score.
Create the product of z-score(price RSI) and z-score(volume RSI), subtract previous day’s value, and rank descending.
Return rolling count of days where absolute return > 1.5 × ATR and volume spike < 1, divided by window length (window = 21).
Produce factor equal to rolling median of (close × volume) divided by rolling MAD of the same, minus its 120-day median, then standardize.
